{{Short description|Film award ceremony in 2006}} {{Use mdy dates|date=April 2025}} {{Infobox film awards | number = 63 | award = Golden Globe Awards | image = | caption = | date = January 16, 2006 |site=[[Beverly Hilton Hotel]]
[[Beverly Hills]], [[Los Angeles]], [[California]]| best_film_drama = ''[[Brokeback Mountain]]'' | best_film_musical_or_comedy = ''[[Walk the Line]]'' | best_drama_series = ''[[Lost (2004 TV series)|Lost]]'' | best_musical_or_comedy_series = ''[[Desperate Housewives]]'' | best_miniseries_or_tv_movie = ''[[Empire Falls (miniseries)|Empire Falls]]'' |most_wins=(4) ''Brokeback Mountain''|most_nominations=(7) ''Brokeback Mountain''| last = 62nd | next = 64th | network = [[NBC]] }} The '''63rd [[Golden Globe Award]]s''', honoring the best in [[film]] and [[television]] for [[2005 in film|2005]], were presented on January 16, 2006, at the [[Beverly Hilton Hotel]], in [[Los Angeles]], [[California]].{{cite news|last1=Waxman|first1=Sharon|title=At the Globes, 'Brokeback Mountain' Takes Top Awards|url=https://www.nytimes.com/2006/01/17/arts/at-the-globes-brokeback-mountain-takes-top-awards.html|access-date=10 January 2018|work=The New York Times|date=17 January 2006}}{{cite news|last1=Horn|first1=John|last2=King|first2=Susan|title='Brokeback' is high in the saddle|url=https://www.latimes.com/archives/la-xpm-2006-jan-17-et-goldenmain17-story.html|access-date=10 January 2018|work=Los Angeles Times|date=17 January 2006}}{{cite web|title=‘Walk the Line,’ ‘Brokeback’ win Golden Globes|url=https://www.today.com/popculture/walk-line-brokeback-win-golden-globes-wbna10878303|access-date=10 January 2018|work=Today.com|date=16 January 2006}} The nominations were announced on December 13, 2005.{{cite web|last1=Silverman|first1=Stephen|title=Brokeback, Housewives Top Globe Nods|url=http://people.com/celebrity/brokeback-housewives-top-globe-nods/|website=People|access-date=10 January 2018|date=13 December 2005}}{{cite news|last1=Vries|first1=Lloyd|title='Brokeback Mountain' Leads Globes|url=https://www.cbsnews.com/news/brokeback-mountain-leads-globes/|access-date=10 January 2018|work=CBS News|date=13 December 2005}}{{cite web|last1=Karger|first1=Dave|title=The Golden Globes start the Oscar race|url=http://ew.com/article/2005/12/16/golden-globes-start-oscar-race/|website=Entertainment Weekly|access-date=10 January 2018|date=16 December 2005}} The ceremony aired on Monday rather than its traditional Sunday after the previous year ceremony's ratings were negatively impacted by the popularity of ''[[Desperate Housewives]]''.{{cite web|title=Globes moved to avoid Housewives|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/2/hi/entertainment/4748401.stm|website=BBC News|date=5 August 2005|access-date=11 January 2016}} ==Winners and nominees== [[File:Philip Seymour Hoffman
